points.text: Lonza announced expansion of payload-linker manufacturing capacity at its Visp site to support the antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) market, which relies on monoclonal antibody manufacture, conjugation, and drug product capabilities.
Ipsen entered a definitive agreement to acquire Memo Therapeutics for >€700M, securing potravitug, a monoclonal antibody against BKPyV for BK polyomavirus-associated nephropathy.
AstraZeneca's tozorakimab, an IL-33 biologic, achieved positive results in its third Phase III trial (MIRANDA), joining OBERON and TITANIA to support regulatory submissions for COPD.

Lonza — Payload-linker manufacturing expansion — ADC therapies — New commercial-scale HPAPI and linker capacity at Visp site; facility integrates with existing monoclonal antibody manufacture and conjugation capabilities; operational expected 2028.
Ipsen — Memo Therapeutics — BK polyomavirus-associated nephropathy — Acquired for >€700M (€200M upfront); deal includes potravitug, a monoclonal antibody evaluated in Phase II (SAFE Kidney II) trial.
AstraZeneca — Tozorakimab — COPD — Three positive Phase III trials (MIRANDA, OBERON, TITANIA) completed; first IL-33 biologic to achieve this milestone, regulatory discussions pending.

Lonza — Integrated ADC ecosystem expansion — Payload-linkers and monoclonal antibodies — Expanding Visp site with new HPAPI capacity, conjugation, and drug product manufacturing; facility designed for multipurpose use and scale-out.
Ipsen — Strategic acquisition of monoclonal antibody assets — Potravitug (BKPyV mAb) — Deal value >€700M; assets transfer expected Q3 2026.
AstraZeneca — Respiratory biologic expansion — Tozorakimab (IL-33 inhibitor) — Three Phase III trials positive; positioned as first-in-class COPD biologic for broad patient populations.

ADC Payload-Linkers — Mechanism: Conjugation to monoclonal antibodies for targeted delivery — Phase: Clinical and commercial manufacturing support — Population: Patients requiring targeted ADC therapies — Endpoints: Enhanced production of complex, highly potent molecules via new Visp facility.

Potravitug — Mechanism: Monoclonal antibody against BK polyomavirus (BKPyV) — Phase: 2 (SAFE Kidney II) — Population: Kidney transplant recipients with BKPyV-associated nephropathy — Comparator: Not specified in source — Status: Under evaluation.

Tozorakimab — Mechanism: Dual inhibition of reduced and oxidized IL-33 — Phase: 3 (MIRANDA, OBERON, TITANIA) — Population: Broad COPD population including smokers and varying eosinophil levels — Comparator: Not specified in source — Results: Positive efficacy across all three trials.

Lonza — Strategic capacity investment — ADC payload-linker and monoclonal antibody manufacturing — Expansion of Visp GMP facility to include new suites for highly potent active pharmaceutical ingredients; expected operational date 2028.
Ipsen — Memo Therapeutics — BK polyomavirus-associated nephropathy — Acquired for >€700M (€200M upfront); deal includes potravitug, a monoclonal antibody evaluated in Phase II (SAFE Kidney II) trial.
